QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_1

question.text: Which exchange rate system was established under the Bretton Woods Agreement of 1944?

answer.text: All currencies were pegged to USD, and USD was pegged to gold
All currencies were pegged to gold directly
All currencies were allowed to float freely
All currencies were pegged to the British Pound

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Bretton Woods System (1944-1971), currencies were pegged to USD at a fixed rate, and USD was convertible to gold. Countries agreed to maintain exchange rates within ±1% of the fixed parity with USD. **The Trap:** 'Pegged to gold directly' confuses Bretton Woods with the earlier Gold Standard. Under Bretton Woods, only USD had a direct gold link — other currencies were pegged to USD. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I (Currency Derivatives) Ch1 covers this in detail. Series XII (Securities Foundation) also tests basic forex concepts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding exchange rate history helps explain why USD remains the dominant vehicle currency in global forex markets today.
